items.headline: AAPL Q2 FY26 actual prints Apr 30 after-close
items.diff_narrative: **New: AAPL Q2 FY26 actual prints Apr 30 after-close — revenue $111.2B (+17% YoY) vs $109.66B estimate; diluted EPS $2.01 vs $1.94 est.; iPhone $57B (+22%, but missed JPM's $59.5B high side); Services record $30.98B (+16%); Greater China $20.5B (+28%, the cleanest segment beat); gross margin 49.3%; $100B buyback authorized + dividend +4% to $0.27/share; Q3 guide revenue +14-17%** — Yesterday's report previewed (consensus EPS $1.95 / rev $109.7B, JP Morgan high-side $112.7B). Today has actuals — Apple beat the consensus revenue and EPS but missed JPM's high-side iPhone number; Greater China was the cleanest single segment beat. The +$100B buyback is the second consecutive FY-Q2 with a $100B authorization. AAPL +2% AH on the print.

items.headline: Apr 30 stock market close
items.diff_narrative: **New: Apr 30 stock market close — S&P 500 7,196.01 (+1.02%) first-ever close above 7,200; Nasdaq +0.89%; both setting new records; April was best month since 2020 (Alphabet +34% / AAPL +5% on the month); Apr 30 leaders Caterpillar (+10%), Eli Lilly (+9%), Alphabet (+9%); Apr 29 print-day losers Meta -7.5%, Microsoft -3.8%** — Yesterday's report had Apr 29 close (S&P 7,135.95 -0.04%, Nasdaq 24,673.24 +0.04%). Today adds the Apr 30 leg + the April monthly recap. The "best month since 2020" frame is the structural number; it dwarfs the Apr 28 OpenAI-revenue-miss + the Mag 7 mixed-print noise.

items.headline: OpenAI on AWS Bedrock launches Apr 28 (limited preview)
items.diff_narrative: **New: OpenAI on AWS Bedrock launches Apr 28 (limited preview) — GPT-5.5 / OpenAI frontier models on Bedrock, Codex on Bedrock, Bedrock Managed Agents powered by OpenAI; Microsoft–OpenAI partnership reset Apr 27 made Microsoft license non-exclusive through 2032; Microsoft no longer pays revenue-share to OpenAI; OpenAI revenue-share to Microsoft continues through 2030 but with a cap; AWS commitments fungible with OpenAI usage** — Yesterday's report did not surface this directly; coverage today is the structural reset. Apple-style staged release (Microsoft reset Apr 27 → Bedrock launch Apr 28). This is OpenAI's first non-Azure home for first-party model serving and a direct counter to the Apr 28 WSJ revenue-miss tape.

items.headline: OpenAI Codex CLI 0.128.0-alpha.1 ships Apr 30
items.diff_narrative: **New: OpenAI Codex CLI 0.128.0-alpha.1 ships Apr 30 — adds `/goal`, in-app browser for local-dev / file-backed pages, Unix-socket app-server transport, sticky environments, remote-thread config plumbing; model providers now own model discovery (AWS/Bedrock account state exposed to app clients); rollout tracing + multi-agent debug reducer + bundled OpenAI Docs skill update for GPT-5.5 / gpt-image-2** — Yesterday's report did not have this. The model-provider-owned discovery + AWS Bedrock account state plumbing is the developer-side companion to the Apr 28 Bedrock launch.

items.headline: OpenAI Agents SDK update Apr 30
items.diff_narrative: **New: OpenAI Agents SDK update Apr 30 — model-native harness, native sandbox execution; sandbox providers Blaxel / Cloudflare / Daytona / E2B / Modal / Runloop / Vercel; Python first, TypeScript planned; configurable memory + sandbox-aware orchestration + Codex-like filesystem tools** — Yesterday's report did not surface this. This is the vendor-side validation of the Apr 28 Novita Sandbox + ATT&CK v19 agentic-AI threat-class — sandbox-execution is now a default primitive across the OpenAI stack with seven third-party sandbox providers as first-class deployment targets.

items.headline: CSAI Foundation Apr 29 milestones
items.diff_narrative: **New: CSAI Foundation Apr 29 milestones — Cloud Security Alliance authorized as a CVE Numbering Authority (CNA) by MITRE for agentic-AI vulnerabilities; STAR for AI Catastrophic Risk Annex (4-phase rollout June 2026 → Dec 2027); Vanta-contributed AARM specification + ATF stewardship transfer from MassiveScale.AI** — Yesterday's report did not have this. The CNA is the first agentic-AI-specific CVE issuer in MITRE's program; this is the institutional landing of the agent-skills threat-class as a first-class disclosure regime, parallel to the Apr 28 ATT&CK v19 named-techniques wave.

items.headline: CISA KEV Apr 30 adds CVE-2026-41940 WebPros cPanel & WHM Missing-Authentication-for-Critical-Function
items.diff_narrative: **New: CISA KEV Apr 30 adds CVE-2026-41940 WebPros cPanel & WHM Missing-Authentication-for-Critical-Function — federal remediation deadline May 21, 2026; sets a fresh KEV-deadline cycle following the Apr 28 KEV adds (CVE-2024-1708 ConnectWise + CVE-2026-32202 Windows Shell, both deadline May 12)** — Yesterday's report had the Apr 28 KEV. Today adds the Apr 30 cPanel & WHM line — cPanel is the management plane for tens of thousands of shared-hosting fleets, so this is the largest single-product KEV addition in the cycle.

items.headline: Apr 30 fresh CVE wave
items.diff_narrative: **New: Apr 30 fresh CVE wave — CVE-2026-7503 (CVSS 8.8), CVE-2026-6389 (CVSS 8.8), CVE-2026-6543 (CVSS 8.8), CVE-2026-7551 (CVSS 8.8); LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 (CVSS 9.3, pre-auth SQLi against /chat/completions Authorization header) carry-forward — patched Apr 19 in v1.83.7-stable, first observed exploitation Apr 26 16:17 UTC (~26h post-advisory)** — Yesterday's report had Apr 28-29 wave + SGLang CVE-2026-5760 carry-forward. Today adds the Apr 30 layer + LiteLLM detail — the LiteLLM gateway is structurally the same shape as Spring AI FilterExpressionConverter (model-tooling-layer injection at the auth/RAG interface) and one row leak compromises OpenAI org keys + Anthropic admin tokens + AWS Bedrock IAM credentials simultaneously.

items.headline: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 GA Apr 3 carry-forward into Apr 28-30 + Foundry Toolkit for VS Code GA + Toolbox in Foundry preview + hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service preview + Observability in Foundry Control Plane GA
items.diff_narrative: **New: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 GA Apr 3 carry-forward into Apr 28-30 + Foundry Toolkit for VS Code GA + Toolbox in Foundry preview + hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service preview + Observability in Foundry Control Plane GA** — Yesterday's report covered Foundry Local GA Apr 9. Today adds the broader Foundry stack — Microsoft's vendor-side answer to the Apr 28 Bedrock AgentCore + Apr 28 Novita Sandbox layer.

items.headline: Powell Apr 29 framing carry into Apr 30
items.diff_narrative: **New: Powell Apr 29 framing carry into Apr 30 — Powell will stay on Board for "a period of time to be determined" past May 15 Chair-term expiration; first Fed Chair since 1948 to do so; rejects "shadow chair" framing; Warsh nomination confirmed by Senate Banking same day, full-Senate vote pending before May 15 handoff** — Yesterday's report had the Apr 29 8-4 FOMC dissent + Powell-staying-on-Board. Today adds the "shadow chair" framing + the 1948 precedent + the Warsh-confirmation timing. This converts the institutional-risk premium from one-day to durable.

items.headline: Big Tech 2026 AI capex tracking $700B per Apr 30 reporting; Alphabet+Meta+Microsoft+Amazon Q1 collective capex >$130B; the structural counter to Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-revenue-miss thesis
items.diff_narrative: **New: Big Tech 2026 AI capex tracking $700B per Apr 30 reporting; Alphabet+Meta+Microsoft+Amazon Q1 collective capex >$130B; the structural counter to Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-revenue-miss thesis** — Yesterday's report did not put a 2026 number on the capex tape. Today does — at $700B the AI-capex-on-faith thesis has a price tag, and the Apr 30 Apple +$100B buyback authorization layered on the same tape is the cohort signal that capex spend is being underwritten by buyback capacity.

items.headline: Apr Jobs Report releases May 1 8:30 AM EDT
items.diff_narrative: **New: Apr Jobs Report releases May 1 8:30 AM EDT — forecast nonfarm payroll 135K vs March 178K; first major macro print into the May 15 Chair handoff window; federal-layoff + tariff drag are the analyst overhangs** — Yesterday's report did not flag the May 1 release. Today does — this is the first non-earnings macro tape into the FOMC succession.

items.headline: Simon Willison Apr 30 weblog wave
items.diff_narrative: **New: Simon Willison Apr 30 weblog wave — "We need RSS for sharing abundant vibe-coded apps", "Codex CLI 0.128.0 adds /goal", "The Zig project's rationale for their firm anti-AI contribution policy", quote from Andrew Kelley on LLM PRs vs human mistakes** — Yesterday's report had Apr 27-29 Simon coverage (AGI-clause unwind retrospective, GPT-5.5 prompting guide, LLM 0.32a0 refactor). Today adds the Apr 30 wave — the Zig anti-AI policy is the structural piece (first major systems-language project to ban LLM-assisted contributions categorically).

items.headline: Google DeepMind "Vision Banana" Apr 30
items.diff_narrative: **New: Google DeepMind "Vision Banana" Apr 30 — single AI system outperforming five specialized models across vision tasks; zero-shot transfer claimed; positioned for robotics + autonomous-vehicle simplification** — Yesterday's report did not have this. The model-class is "single-vision-foundation replacing the perception stack pipeline"; pairs with the broader 2026 Apr-Jun Google Apr 22 Anthropic-investment / cloud-agent push as evidence Google is leaning into the universal-perception-foundation play.

items.headline: SoftBank Roze AI Apr 30
items.diff_narrative: **New: SoftBank Roze AI Apr 30 — robotics startup automating data center construction across the US with autonomous robots streamlining server-farm builds** — Yesterday's report did not have this. Pairs with the $700B 2026 AI-capex tape — the data-center buildout itself becomes the first physical-AI commercial deployment beyond logistics + automotive.

items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: Apr 28 WSJ OpenAI-revenue-miss + Apr 29 Mag 7 super-week (MSFT/GOOGL/META/AMZN) + Apr 28 Microsoft–OpenAI partnership reset + Apr 28 OpenAI-on-Bedrock — all carry into the May tape** — Carry-forward; the Apr 30 Apple beat + $700B 2026 capex tape + record-S&P close adds the institutional ratification of the buyside-pressure thesis.
